title: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DC ID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

SUBJECT: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DCID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

To the FOIA Officer, Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking (DISB):

Pursuant to the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am requesting expedited access to the following public records regarding a consumer complaint I filed against City Lending, Inc. in the fall of 2019. 

The complaint was assigned DCID #2059580 and was handled by Consumer Services Specialist Shahidah S. Williams, JD, within the Compliance Analysis Division. 

On November 6, 2019, Ms. Williams stated in writing that she was administratively closing my complaint and promised that an "official letter" would be generated. However, the core issues of predatory payoff escalations and the withholding of monthly statements were never resolved by your agency.

I demand the immediate production of the following precise records generated between September 1, 2019, and December 31, 2019:

1. The Official Closeout Letter: The formal administrative closeout letter referenced by Shahidah Williams on November 6, 2019, regarding the termination of complaint DCID #2059580.
2. Investigative Notes & Justification: All internal memoranda, case notes, or communications authored by Shahidah Williams justifying the closure of this complaint while severe discrepancies remained between the payoff provided by City Lending and the escalated payoff provided by Samuel I. White, P.C.
3. Supervisory Authorization: All records showing supervisory review and approval authorizing Shahidah Williams to administratively close DCID #2059580.
4. City Lending Communications: All emails, letters, and notes of telephone calls between Shahidah Williams and any representative of City Lending, Inc. (specifically Jorge Campodonico or Stephanie Simmons) regarding their failure to provide the consumer with monthly billing statements and their refusal to update the consumer's mailing address.
5. Escalation & Disciplinary Action (Predatory Payoff): All records, internal memoranda, and enforcement referrals generated by Shahidah Williams, her supervisors, or the Compliance Analysis Division detailing how the initial fabricated payoff amounts submitted by City Lending were escalated for regulatory review, and any subsequent disciplinary or enforcement actions taken against the lender.

I request these records be provided in their native, electronic format with all metadata intact. Because these records involve documented regulatory abandonment and subsequent financial harm to a District resident, I expect a comprehensive search that goes beyond a surface-level query.

Please provide a Vaughn Index for any documents or portions of documents that you claim are exempt from disclosure. 

Sincerely,

Fred Gamble Jr.
1490 Morris Rd SE
Washington, DC 20020

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Correspondence of the members of the VMI Women Faculty Working Group on the subject of the VMI Women's Association for the period 1 May 2026 to the date of this FOIA and any emails on this subject to or from The VMI Dean and/or the VMI DEI Office from the VMI Women's Faculty Working group and/or Valentia Dimitrova-Grazjl, Department of Economics and Business.

It is known that group emails include a proposal for the VMI Women's Association, which was mentioned in a list of "accomplishments" and "work in progress" sent to at least the Dean, and that a proposal for the VMI  Women's Association was sent to the Dean.

These documents are known to exist, although the exact date of the proposal to the Dean for the VMI Women's Association is unknown to me. Therefore, the timeframe is estimated and specifically requested to include said proposal or its current draft.

This is a narrowly scoped request, and VMI should be able to fill it within the 5-day FOIA timeline.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Anonymous.

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Communication with HAND (updated)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All communications between the City of Detroit and Homeless Action Network of Detroit (HAND) referencing grievance complaints from Jan. 1, 2025 to present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Law enforcement impeachment information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

AM REQUESTING THE SPARKS CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE "BRADY LIST" TO INCLUDE ANY PAST OR PRESENT LISTS IN THE POSSESION OF THE CITY OF SPARKS ATTORNEY'S OFFICE

The requested documents related to law enforcement impeachment information in the control and custody of the City Of Sparks Attorney

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant
